name: The Hunted
caption: DC20 Playtest Adventure
description:
This adventure is designed to be setting agnostic and is presented in a way to ease those dropping this introductory story into any world.

Party Level: This adventure is written for 1st-level PCs, though it can be tailored to fit slightly higher-level characters with a few tweaks.

system.journal:
Description: This creature is an enormous reptilian beast standing 10 feet tall even while hunched over. A massive tail, powerful hind legs, and long thin limbs make up the bulk of its form while a crocodile-like snout extends past a large leathery pouch. Its throat swells when it lets out croaks and strong, bellowing roars.

Tactics: When surrounded by its young, it’ll croak to command them (see Inspiring Croak and Coordinated Assault). On the offensive, it ambushes and attacks from a distance, then swallows weak enemies (see Tongue). It jumps toward ranged attackers to close distances, then backs away when melee attackers get close. If it can’t see any viable target, it uses its Echo to feel out its surroundings.

Lore: The Krasis is a powerful predator. Hunting one is dangerous, but its acid sack is prized for use in many potions and poisons.

Token created with TokenStamp 2.

System Tip: This is not automated.

system.description:
When you are hit by a Ranged Attack from a creature within 5 Spaces, you can leap on top of that creature, landing in a Space adjacent to the attacker. The target must make a Might Save.

Failure: Target is knocked Prone

Melee Martial Attack vs PD, 5 Spaces, 1 Bludgeoning and target makes an Agility Save.

Failure: Target is pulled towards you and Consumed.

You can’t use this Attack while you have a creature Consumed.

While Consumed: It’s Restrained and takes 1 Corrosion damage at the end of its turns.

Escape Consume: Each time you lose 5 HP, you must make a DC 15 Might Save.

Failure: The target is vomited into an adjacent Space and is no longer Restrained.

System Tip: Use on demand macro to roll the save.

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

system.description:
Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to push a creature within 1 Space of you. Make an Athletics Check contested by the target’s Martial Check.

Success: You push the creature 1 Space away from you or to its left or right.

Success (each 5): Push up to 1 additional Space.

Knock Prone: After the result, you can choose to reduce the total distance the target is pushed by 1 Space to knock them Prone instead.

DC Tip: If you shove a creature into a target, follow the rules for hitting the target in the Collision section.

If you move at least 2 Spaces in a straight line, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Tackle a creature that’s your size or smaller.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the target's Martial Check.

Success: You Grapple the target and you both move 1 Space in the same direction you were moving then immediately fall Prone.

Success (each 5): +1 Space moved.

You can spend 1 AP to throw an object or a creature you have Grappled.

Object

The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table. For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically.

Throw at a Target: When you throw an object at a target it’s considered a Ranged Martial Attack with an Improvised Weapon against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The target takes 1 damage of a type determined by the GM.

  • Miss: The thrown object continues traveling up to your maximum throwing distance.

DC Tip: When you throw Weapons (including an Improvised Weapon) that lack the Toss or Thrown Properties as part of an Attack, you make the Attack with DisADV.

Creature

Make an Athletics Check against the target’s Might Save.

  • Check Success: The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table (ending the Grapple).

For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically upward.

Throw at a Target: When you throw the creature at a target, you compare your Athletics Check against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The thrown creature and the target share the Collision damage (each take half).

  • Miss: The thrown creature continues traveling to your maximum throwing distance.

Example: A Medium sized creature with a Might of 0 (minimum Might of 1) could throw a Tiny object up to 2 Spaces, a Small object 1 Space, and Medium object 1 Space (rounded up from 0.5). Conversely, if that creature had a Might of 6, they could throw a Tiny object 12 Spaces, a Small object 6 Spaces, and a Medium object 3 Spaces.

System Info: In the DC20 system, throwing is not a basic action and has its own section in the rules. For automation purposes, I added it to the actions.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to Hide from 1 or more creatures that can’t see you (Unseen).

Make a Stealth Check against the opposing creature’(s) Passive Awareness.

Success: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat (making you Unseen and Unheard by them).

You remain Hidden until you make a noise louder than a whisper, make an Attack, cast a Spell with a Verbal Component, or a creature takes the Search Action and successfully locates you.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

effects.name: Hidden
effects.description: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to move up to your Speed in Spaces (default of 5).

You choose where to move, and can break up your movement by moving before and after taking a different Action.

You can’t end your turn in a Space occupied by another creature.

A creature with 4 AP can take the Move Action to move 1 Space, take the Attack Action, move 1 Space again (2 total Spaces), take the Attack Action again, and then move 2 Spaces again (4 total Spaces).

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to grant another creature a d8 Help Die that lasts until the start of your next turn. Upon granting the Help Die, you must declare which creature you’re Helping and the type of Check you’ll be aiding them with while meeting the following conditions:

  • Attack: You declare 1 target for the Attack. You must be within 1 Space of the Attacker or the target of the Attack. While the Help Die lasts, it can be added to an Attack made against the target.

  • Skill or Trade Check: You declare a type of Skill or Trade Check. You describe how you’re Helping them and must do so with a Skill or Trade that you have at least 1 Mastery Level in. You can use the same Skill or Trade or a different one.

The Help Die can only be used to aid the type of Check declared and disappears once it is used.

Multiple Help Penalty: Once you take the Help Action, each time you take the Help Action again before the end of your turn, your Help Die decays by 1 step, to a minimum of a d4 (d8 | d6 | d4). These Help Dice only decay when using the Help Action. Help Die granted by other sources (such as the Sword Maneuver) decay independently of any Help Dice grant through the Help Action.

You can spend 1 AP to perform 1 of the following object interactions:

  • Drink a Potion or administer a Potion to another creature.

  • Attempt to lock or unlock a Lock.

  • Make a Trickery Check to activate or disable a trap or other mechanism.

  • Transfer an item to or from another creature (only 1 of the two creatures spends 1 AP).
You can spend 1 or more AP to cast a Spell that you know. If the Spell has a Mana Point requirement, you must spend that much MP to cast the Spell.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to recall or discern some information about a creature that you can see or hear. Make a DC 10 Knowledge Check.

Success: You learn a piece of lore about the creature.

Success (each 5): You learn 1 creature statistic (PD, AD, Attacks, Abilities, Resistances, Vulnerabilities, Immunities, etc.).

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to beguile a Beast that can see or hear you. Make an Animal Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The animal is Taunted by you for 1 minute (Repeated Save) or until you target it with a harmful Attack, Spell, or other effect.

Success (5): It’s also Impaired.

Success (10): It’s also Charmed.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to discern the course of actions a creature might take on its next turn.

Make an Insight Check contested by the target’s Trickery or Influence Check (its choice).

Success: You learn the target’s emotional state and whether it plans to make an Attack, cast a Spell, or flee combat during its next turn.

Success (5): You know who the creature is likely to target with a harmful ability.

Success (10): You know which ability the creature plans to use.

If the target is a PC, its player tells you what the creature plans to do. If the target is an NPC, the GM tells you what the creature plans to do.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to hide an object on yourself or in nearby foliage, debris, or decor to render it Hidden.

Make a contested Trickery Check against the Passive Awareness of creatures that can see you.

Success: The object is Hidden from any creature whose Passive Awareness you beat.

You can spend 1 AP to make a Trickery Check Contested by the target’s Insight Check.

Success: The next Attack against the target before the start of your next turn has ADV and deals +1 damage.

effects.name: Feint
effects.description: The next Attack against you has ADV and deals +1 damage.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to intimidate a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Intimidation Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Intimidated by you until the end of your next turn.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

When you perform a Jump, you can spend 1 AP to increase your Jump Distance for the Jump.

Make a DC 10 Acrobatics Check.

  • Failure: Your Jump Distance increases by 1.

  • Success: Your Jump Distance increases by 2.

  • Success (each 5): +1 Jump Distance.

DC Tip: Remember, your Long Jump Distance equals your Jump Distance in Spaces, and your High Jump distance equals your Jump Distance in feet.

You can spend 1 AP to touch a creature and tend to its wounds.

Make a DC 10 Medicine Check.

Success: You stop its Bleeding or Stabilize it (your choice).

Success (each 5): The creature gains +1 Temp HP.

You can spend 1 AP to maneuver a mount you’re riding to avoid danger.

Make a DC 10 Animal Check.

Success: The mount’s PD increases by 2 until the start of your next turn.

Success (5): +2 PD.

Success (10): +4 PD.

effects.description: The mount’s PD increases by 4 until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Mounted Defense +4
effects.description: The mount’s PD increases by 6 until the start of your next turn.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to move through a Space occupied by a hostile creature that’s within 1 size of you.

Make a contested Martial Check against the target.

Success: You can move through the creature’s Space as if it were Difficult Terrain (Slowed while moving through the area).

Success (5): You can move through the creature's space without it counting as Difficult Terrain.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to locate 1 or more Hidden creatures and concealed objects within your Line of Sight.

  • Hidden Creatures: You attempt to locate any Hidden creatures in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the Stealth Check of any Hidden creatures.

    Success: You know the location of any Hidden creature whose Stealth Check you beat until the end of your turn. Knowing a creature’s location does not grant you the ability to see it.

  • Hidden Objects: You attempt to locate any Hidden objects in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the DC to discover any concealed objects (such as traps, secret doors, or hidden items).

    Success: You discover the location of any Hidden object whose discovery DC you beat.

Without obstruction, a PC’s line of sight could reach the horizon. You might ask your PC to make their Awareness Check with DisADV to detect a creature that’s far away, such as beyond 10 Spaces.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to taunt a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Influence Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Taunted by you for 1 Round.

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

Prerequisite: Spellcasting Path

During a Spell Duel, the creature casting the Spell is referred to as the Caster and the opposing creature is referred to as the Challenger.

If multiple creatures attempt to initiate a Spell Duel, one of them must be chosen as the Challenger, while the others assist them through Combo Casting. Additional creatures can also choose to assist the Caster when a Spell Duel is initiated (see Combo Casting for more information).

Trigger: You see an MP Effect being cast, provided the Caster or a target is within 5 Spaces of you.

Reaction: You can spend an amount of AP and 1 or more MP (up to your Mana Spend Limit) to attempt to stop the Caster’s MP Effect. The amount of AP spent is equal to the base AP cost of the MP Effect you are trying to stop.

Contest

Make a Spell Check contested by the Spell Check or Spell Attack that the Caster makes to cast their MP Effect.

If the Caster’s MP Effect doesn’t require a Check, they have to make a Spell Check as a Reaction as part of the Spell Duel.

For the purposes of the Spell Duel, the Caster and Challenger each gain a bonus to their Check equal to twice the MP spent they spent respectively.

Beta Note: Since enemies don’t have MP costs for their MP Effects you can assume that their MP Effects cost an amount of MP equal to half their Level (rounded up).

Outcomes

Success: The Caster’s MP Effect fails and has no effect.

Failure: The Caster’s MP Effect succeeds and takes effect. The Check bonus gained from MP spent doesn’t apply to the MP Effect’s Success and Failure Statements.

Tie: The Caster’s MP Effect fails, has no effect, and you each roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.roll-tables.RollTable.HxkcpDT6pEOIfYUk]{Wild Magic Surge Table}. The effect from the table lasts for 1 Round.

Resources Lost: Whatever the result, each creature still spends all AP, MP, or other resources as normal.

Example: A Caster makes an MP based Spell Attack and a Challenger attempts to stop it. The Caster’s Spell Attack cost 1 AP and 2 MP. The Challenger must spend 1 AP and at least 1 MP to attempt to stop the Spell Attack. The Caster makes an Attack Check (since its a Spell Attack) and the Challenger makes a Spell Check. The Caster gets a +4 bonus to their Attack Check (2 MP spent) and the Challenger gets a +2 bonus to their Spell Check (1 MP spent). Should the Caster win the Contest, their MP Effect takes effect but they don’t include the +4 bonus from MP spent to determine if they score a Heavy Hit or higher.

system.description:
You must spend 1 AP at the start of each of your turns to Sustain an effect until the start of your next turn.

You can't Sustain an effect while Dazed. When you become Dazed, any effect you’re Sustaining ends. You can choose to end the effect at anytime for free.

Outside of Combat: You can only Sustain 1 effect at a time outside of Combat. If you attempt to Sustain a second effect while already Sustaining a first, the first effect immediately ends.
Give +1 Temp HP to creatures of your choice within 5 Spaces (including yourself).

If you don’t have a creature Consumed, use Tongue once for free.

If you do, jump up to your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

Area Spell Attack vs AD, 3 Space Cone, 2 Corrosion and target makes a Repeated Agility Save.

Failure: Target no longer benefits from PDR or EDR for 1 minute

effects.name: Dissolved
effects.description: You no longer benefit from PDR or EDR for 1 minute.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to Hide from 1 or more creatures that can’t see you (Unseen).

Make a Stealth Check against the opposing creature’(s) Passive Awareness.

Success: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat (making you Unseen and Unheard by them).

You remain Hidden until you make a noise louder than a whisper, make an Attack, cast a Spell with a Verbal Component, or a creature takes the Search Action and successfully locates you.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

You choose where to move, and can break up your movement by moving before and after taking a different Action.

You can’t end your turn in a Space occupied by another creature.

A creature with 4 AP can take the Move Action to move 1 Space, take the Attack Action, move 1 Space again (2 total Spaces), take the Attack Action again, and then move 2 Spaces again (4 total Spaces).

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to grant another creature a d8 Help Die that lasts until the start of your next turn. Upon granting the Help Die, you must declare which creature you’re Helping and the type of Check you’ll be aiding them with while meeting the following conditions:

  • Attack: You declare 1 target for the Attack. You must be within 1 Space of the Attacker or the target of the Attack. While the Help Die lasts, it can be added to an Attack made against the target.

  • Skill or Trade Check: You declare a type of Skill or Trade Check. You describe how you’re Helping them and must do so with a Skill or Trade that you have at least 1 Mastery Level in. You can use the same Skill or Trade or a different one.

The Help Die can only be used to aid the type of Check declared and disappears once it is used.

Multiple Help Penalty: Once you take the Help Action, each time you take the Help Action again before the end of your turn, your Help Die decays by 1 step, to a minimum of a d4 (d8 | d6 | d4). These Help Dice only decay when using the Help Action. Help Die granted by other sources (such as the Sword Maneuver) decay independently of any Help Dice grant through the Help Action.

You can spend 1 AP to perform 1 of the following object interactions:

  • Drink a Potion or administer a Potion to another creature.

  • Attempt to lock or unlock a Lock.

  • Make a Trickery Check to activate or disable a trap or other mechanism.

  • Transfer an item to or from another creature (only 1 of the two creatures spends 1 AP).
You can spend 1 or more AP to cast a Spell that you know. If the Spell has a Mana Point requirement, you must spend that much MP to cast the Spell.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to recall or discern some information about a creature that you can see or hear. Make a DC 10 Knowledge Check.

Success: You learn a piece of lore about the creature.

Success (each 5): You learn 1 creature statistic (PD, AD, Attacks, Abilities, Resistances, Vulnerabilities, Immunities, etc.).

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to beguile a Beast that can see or hear you. Make an Animal Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The animal is Taunted by you for 1 minute (Repeated Save) or until you target it with a harmful Attack, Spell, or other effect.

Success (5): It’s also Impaired.

Success (10): It’s also Charmed.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to discern the course of actions a creature might take on its next turn.

Make an Insight Check contested by the target’s Trickery or Influence Check (its choice).

Success: You learn the target’s emotional state and whether it plans to make an Attack, cast a Spell, or flee combat during its next turn.

Success (5): You know who the creature is likely to target with a harmful ability.

Success (10): You know which ability the creature plans to use.

If the target is a PC, its player tells you what the creature plans to do. If the target is an NPC, the GM tells you what the creature plans to do.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to hide an object on yourself or in nearby foliage, debris, or decor to render it Hidden.

Make a contested Trickery Check against the Passive Awareness of creatures that can see you.

Success: The object is Hidden from any creature whose Passive Awareness you beat.

You can spend 1 AP to make a Trickery Check Contested by the target’s Insight Check.

Success: The next Attack against the target before the start of your next turn has ADV and deals +1 damage.

effects.name: Feint
effects.description: The next Attack against you has ADV and deals +1 damage.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to intimidate a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Intimidation Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Intimidated by you until the end of your next turn.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

When you perform a Jump, you can spend 1 AP to increase your Jump Distance for the Jump.

Make a DC 10 Acrobatics Check.

  • Failure: Your Jump Distance increases by 1.

  • Success: Your Jump Distance increases by 2.

  • Success (each 5): +1 Jump Distance.

DC Tip: Remember, your Long Jump Distance equals your Jump Distance in Spaces, and your High Jump distance equals your Jump Distance in feet.

You can spend 1 AP to touch a creature and tend to its wounds.

Make a DC 10 Medicine Check.

Success: You stop its Bleeding or Stabilize it (your choice).

Success (each 5): The creature gains +1 Temp HP.

You can spend 1 AP to maneuver a mount you’re riding to avoid danger.

Make a DC 10 Animal Check.

Success: The mount’s PD increases by 2 until the start of your next turn.

Success (5): +2 PD.

Success (10): +4 PD.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to move through a Space occupied by a hostile creature that’s within 1 size of you.

Make a contested Martial Check against the target.

Success: You can move through the creature’s Space as if it were Difficult Terrain (Slowed while moving through the area).

Success (5): You can move through the creature's space without it counting as Difficult Terrain.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to locate 1 or more Hidden creatures and concealed objects within your Line of Sight.

  • Hidden Creatures: You attempt to locate any Hidden creatures in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the Stealth Check of any Hidden creatures.

    Success: You know the location of any Hidden creature whose Stealth Check you beat until the end of your turn. Knowing a creature’s location does not grant you the ability to see it.

  • Hidden Objects: You attempt to locate any Hidden objects in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the DC to discover any concealed objects (such as traps, secret doors, or hidden items).

    Success: You discover the location of any Hidden object whose discovery DC you beat.

Without obstruction, a PC’s line of sight could reach the horizon. You might ask your PC to make their Awareness Check with DisADV to detect a creature that’s far away, such as beyond 10 Spaces.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to taunt a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Influence Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Taunted by you for 1 Round.

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

Prerequisite: Spellcasting Path

During a Spell Duel, the creature casting the Spell is referred to as the Caster and the opposing creature is referred to as the Challenger.

If multiple creatures attempt to initiate a Spell Duel, one of them must be chosen as the Challenger, while the others assist them through Combo Casting. Additional creatures can also choose to assist the Caster when a Spell Duel is initiated (see Combo Casting for more information).

Trigger: You see an MP Effect being cast, provided the Caster or a target is within 5 Spaces of you.

Reaction: You can spend an amount of AP and 1 or more MP (up to your Mana Spend Limit) to attempt to stop the Caster’s MP Effect. The amount of AP spent is equal to the base AP cost of the MP Effect you are trying to stop.

Contest

Make a Spell Check contested by the Spell Check or Spell Attack that the Caster makes to cast their MP Effect.

If the Caster’s MP Effect doesn’t require a Check, they have to make a Spell Check as a Reaction as part of the Spell Duel.

For the purposes of the Spell Duel, the Caster and Challenger each gain a bonus to their Check equal to twice the MP spent they spent respectively.

Beta Note: Since enemies don’t have MP costs for their MP Effects you can assume that their MP Effects cost an amount of MP equal to half their Level (rounded up).

Outcomes

Success: The Caster’s MP Effect fails and has no effect.

Failure: The Caster’s MP Effect succeeds and takes effect. The Check bonus gained from MP spent doesn’t apply to the MP Effect’s Success and Failure Statements.

Tie: The Caster’s MP Effect fails, has no effect, and you each roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.roll-tables.RollTable.HxkcpDT6pEOIfYUk]{Wild Magic Surge Table}. The effect from the table lasts for 1 Round.

Resources Lost: Whatever the result, each creature still spends all AP, MP, or other resources as normal.

Example: A Caster makes an MP based Spell Attack and a Challenger attempts to stop it. The Caster’s Spell Attack cost 1 AP and 2 MP. The Challenger must spend 1 AP and at least 1 MP to attempt to stop the Spell Attack. The Caster makes an Attack Check (since its a Spell Attack) and the Challenger makes a Spell Check. The Caster gets a +4 bonus to their Attack Check (2 MP spent) and the Challenger gets a +2 bonus to their Spell Check (1 MP spent). Should the Caster win the Contest, their MP Effect takes effect but they don’t include the +4 bonus from MP spent to determine if they score a Heavy Hit or higher.

You must spend 1 AP at the start of each of your turns to Sustain an effect until the start of your next turn.

You can't Sustain an effect while Dazed. When you become Dazed, any effect you’re Sustaining ends. You can choose to end the effect at anytime for free.

Outside of Combat: You can only Sustain 1 effect at a time outside of Combat. If you attempt to Sustain a second effect while already Sustaining a first, the first effect immediately ends.

You have DisADV on Attacks against a creature not revealed by Echo, unless they’ve Attacked you since the end of your last turn.

system.journal:
Description: A large pile of muscles equipped with thick furs and iron knuckles, Buff is a Giantborn Halfling usually sporting a vacant stare with a slack jaw. He’s as thickskinned as a rhinoceros and as smart as one, too.

Tactics: Prioritizes Grappling weak and slow opponents, then Slamming them. He’ll use powerful punches (Knuckles Attack) on anyone he’s not Grappling.

Lore: Buff is the Barbarian muscle of The Shadows, a rather disreputable adventuring group. He joined the group with his brother Gruff, the group’s second-in-command. Buff got his mother’s large size and slow wit.

Token Create with TokenStamp 2.

When you make a Melee Martial Attack on your turn, you can move up to 2 Spaces immediately before making the Attack.

System Tip: Use this item to apply add Move Points.

You can spend 1 AP to throw an object or a creature you have Grappled.

Object

The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table. For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically.

Throw at a Target: When you throw an object at a target it’s considered a Ranged Martial Attack with an Improvised Weapon against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The target takes 1 damage of a type determined by the GM.

  • Miss: The thrown object continues traveling up to your maximum throwing distance.

DC Tip: When you throw Weapons (including an Improvised Weapon) that lack the Toss or Thrown Properties as part of an Attack, you make the Attack with DisADV.

Creature

Make an Athletics Check against the target’s Might Save.

  • Check Success: The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table (ending the Grapple).

For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically upward.

Throw at a Target: When you throw the creature at a target, you compare your Athletics Check against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The thrown creature and the target share the Collision damage (each take half).

  • Miss: The thrown creature continues traveling to your maximum throwing distance.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to make an Attack Check Contested by the target's Athletics, Acrobatics, or Trickery Check (target’s choice).

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to push a creature within 1 Space of you. Make an Athletics Check contested by the target’s Martial Check.

Success: You push the creature 1 Space away from you or to its left or right.

Success (each 5): Push up to 1 additional Space.

Knock Prone: After the result, you can choose to reduce the total distance the target is pushed by 1 Space to knock them Prone instead.

DC Tip: If you shove a creature into a target, follow the rules for hitting the target in the Collision section.

You can spend 1 AP to grant another creature a d8 Help Die that lasts until the start of your next turn. Upon granting the Help Die, you must declare which creature you’re Helping and the type of Check you’ll be aiding them with while meeting the following conditions:

  • Attack: You declare 1 target for the Attack. You must be within 1 Space of the Attacker or the target of the Attack. While the Help Die lasts, it can be added to an Attack made against the target.

  • Skill or Trade Check: You declare a type of Skill or Trade Check. You describe how you’re Helping them and must do so with a Skill or Trade that you have at least 1 Mastery Level in. You can use the same Skill or Trade or a different one.

The Help Die can only be used to aid the type of Check declared and disappears once it is used.

Multiple Help Penalty: Once you take the Help Action, each time you take the Help Action again before the end of your turn, your Help Die decays by 1 step, to a minimum of a d4 (d8 | d6 | d4). These Help Dice only decay when using the Help Action. Help Die granted by other sources (such as the Sword Maneuver) decay independently of any Help Dice grant through the Help Action.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to recall or discern some information about a creature that you can see or hear. Make a DC 10 Knowledge Check.

Success: You learn a piece of lore about the creature.

Success (each 5): You learn 1 creature statistic (PD, AD, Attacks, Abilities, Resistances, Vulnerabilities, Immunities, etc.).

If you move at least 2 Spaces in a straight line, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Tackle a creature that’s your size or smaller.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the target's Martial Check.

Success: You Grapple the target and you both move 1 Space in the same direction you were moving then immediately fall Prone.

Success (each 5): +1 Space moved.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to move through a Space occupied by a hostile creature that’s within 1 size of you.

Make a contested Martial Check against the target.

Success: You can move through the creature’s Space as if it were Difficult Terrain (Slowed while moving through the area).

Success (5): You can move through the creature's space without it counting as Difficult Terrain.

You must spend 1 AP at the start of each of your turns to Sustain an effect until the start of your next turn.

You can't Sustain an effect while Dazed. When you become Dazed, any effect you’re Sustaining ends. You can choose to end the effect at anytime for free.

Outside of Combat: You can only Sustain 1 effect at a time outside of Combat. If you attempt to Sustain a second effect while already Sustaining a first, the first effect immediately ends.

You can spend 1 AP to perform 1 of the following object interactions:

  • Drink a Potion or administer a Potion to another creature.

  • Attempt to lock or unlock a Lock.

  • Make a Trickery Check to activate or disable a trap or other mechanism.

  • Transfer an item to or from another creature (only 1 of the two creatures spends 1 AP).

You can spend 1 AP to make a Trickery Check Contested by the target’s Insight Check.

Success: The next Attack against the target before the start of your next turn has ADV and deals +1 damage.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to taunt a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Influence Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Taunted by you for 1 Round.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to intimidate a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Intimidation Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Intimidated by you until the end of your next turn.

When you perform a Jump, you can spend 1 AP to increase your Jump Distance for the Jump.

Make a DC 10 Acrobatics Check.

  • Failure: Your Jump Distance increases by 1.

  • Success: Your Jump Distance increases by 2.

  • Success (each 5): +1 Jump Distance.

DC Tip: Remember, your Long Jump Distance equals your Jump Distance in Spaces, and your High Jump distance equals your Jump Distance in feet.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to locate 1 or more Hidden creatures and concealed objects within your Line of Sight.

  • Hidden Creatures: You attempt to locate any Hidden creatures in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the Stealth Check of any Hidden creatures.

    Success: You know the location of any Hidden creature whose Stealth Check you beat until the end of your turn. Knowing a creature’s location does not grant you the ability to see it.

  • Hidden Objects: You attempt to locate any Hidden objects in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the DC to discover any concealed objects (such as traps, secret doors, or hidden items).

    Success: You discover the location of any Hidden object whose discovery DC you beat.

Without obstruction, a PC’s line of sight could reach the horizon. You might ask your PC to make their Awareness Check with DisADV to detect a creature that’s far away, such as beyond 10 Spaces.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to beguile a Beast that can see or hear you. Make an Animal Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The animal is Taunted by you for 1 minute (Repeated Save) or until you target it with a harmful Attack, Spell, or other effect.

Success (5): It’s also Impaired.

Success (10): It’s also Charmed.

Make a contested Trickery Check against the Passive Awareness of creatures that can see you.

Make a DC 10 Medicine Check.

Success: You stop its Bleeding or Stabilize it (your choice).

Success (each 5): The creature gains +1 Temp HP.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to discern the course of actions a creature might take on its next turn.

Make an Insight Check contested by the target’s Trickery or Influence Check (its choice).

Success: You learn the target’s emotional state and whether it plans to make an Attack, cast a Spell, or flee combat during its next turn.

Success (5): You know who the creature is likely to target with a harmful ability.

Success (10): You know which ability the creature plans to use.

If the target is a PC, its player tells you what the creature plans to do. If the target is an NPC, the GM tells you what the creature plans to do.

system.journal:
Description: Gruff is a small Halfling Giantborn man with an open flowing shirt. He’s usually smirking with an air of superiority.

Tactics: Prioritizes knocking an enemy Prone, particularly those Grappled by an ally, then attacking them.

Lore: Gruff, a Monk, is the second in command of The Shadows, a rather disreputable adventuring group. He joined the group with his brother Buff, the group’s muscle. Gruff got his father’s small size and sly charm.

Token Create with TokenStamp 2.

system.description: You deal +1 damage against creatures that are Prone or Grappled.
When a creature enters your Melee Range, you can make an Opportunity Attack against it.
You can spend 1 AP to make 1 Attack Check.

You can spend 1 AP to make an Attack Check Contested by the target's Athletics, Acrobatics, or Trickery Check (target’s choice).

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

system.description:
Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to push a creature within 1 Space of you. Make an Athletics Check contested by the target’s Martial Check.

Success: You push the creature 1 Space away from you or to its left or right.

Success (each 5): Push up to 1 additional Space.

Knock Prone: After the result, you can choose to reduce the total distance the target is pushed by 1 Space to knock them Prone instead.

DC Tip: If you shove a creature into a target, follow the rules for hitting the target in the Collision section.

If you move at least 2 Spaces in a straight line, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Tackle a creature that’s your size or smaller.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the target's Martial Check.

Success: You Grapple the target and you both move 1 Space in the same direction you were moving then immediately fall Prone.

Success (each 5): +1 Space moved.

You can spend 1 AP to throw an object or a creature you have Grappled.

Object

The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table. For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically.

Throw at a Target: When you throw an object at a target it’s considered a Ranged Martial Attack with an Improvised Weapon against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The target takes 1 damage of a type determined by the GM.

  • Miss: The thrown object continues traveling up to your maximum throwing distance.

DC Tip: When you throw Weapons (including an Improvised Weapon) that lack the Toss or Thrown Properties as part of an Attack, you make the Attack with DisADV.

Creature

Make an Athletics Check against the target’s Might Save.

  • Check Success: The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table (ending the Grapple).

For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically upward.

Throw at a Target: When you throw the creature at a target, you compare your Athletics Check against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The thrown creature and the target share the Collision damage (each take half).

  • Miss: The thrown creature continues traveling to your maximum throwing distance.

Throwing Table

Size

Spaces Thrown

2+ Sizes smaller

2 x Might

1 Size smaller

Might

Same Size

1/2 Might

Larger

Impossible

Example: A Medium sized creature with a Might of 0 (minimum Might of 1) could throw a Tiny object up to 2 Spaces, a Small object 1 Space, and Medium object 1 Space (rounded up from 0.5). Conversely, if that creature had a Might of 6, they could throw a Tiny object 12 Spaces, a Small object 6 Spaces, and a Medium object 3 Spaces.

System Info: In the DC20 system, throwing is not a basic action and has its own section in the rules. For automation purposes, I added it to the actions.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to Hide from 1 or more creatures that can’t see you (Unseen).

Make a Stealth Check against the opposing creature’(s) Passive Awareness.

Success: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat (making you Unseen and Unheard by them).

You remain Hidden until you make a noise louder than a whisper, make an Attack, cast a Spell with a Verbal Component, or a creature takes the Search Action and successfully locates you.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

effects.name: Hidden
effects.description: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to move up to your Speed in Spaces (default of 5).

You choose where to move, and can break up your movement by moving before and after taking a different Action.

You can’t end your turn in a Space occupied by another creature.

A creature with 4 AP can take the Move Action to move 1 Space, take the Attack Action, move 1 Space again (2 total Spaces), take the Attack Action again, and then move 2 Spaces again (4 total Spaces).

During a Spell Duel, the creature casting the Spell is referred to as the Caster and the opposing creature is referred to as the Challenger.

If multiple creatures attempt to initiate a Spell Duel, one of them must be chosen as the Challenger, while the others assist them through Combo Casting. Additional creatures can also choose to assist the Caster when a Spell Duel is initiated (see Combo Casting for more information).

Trigger: You see an MP Effect being cast, provided the Caster or a target is within 5 Spaces of you.

Reaction: You can spend an amount of AP and 1 or more MP (up to your Mana Spend Limit) to attempt to stop the Caster’s MP Effect. The amount of AP spent is equal to the base AP cost of the MP Effect you are trying to stop.

Contest

Make a Spell Check contested by the Spell Check or Spell Attack that the Caster makes to cast their MP Effect.

If the Caster’s MP Effect doesn’t require a Check, they have to make a Spell Check as a Reaction as part of the Spell Duel.

For the purposes of the Spell Duel, the Caster and Challenger each gain a bonus to their Check equal to twice the MP spent they spent respectively.

Beta Note: Since enemies don’t have MP costs for their MP Effects you can assume that their MP Effects cost an amount of MP equal to half their Level (rounded up).

Outcomes

Success: The Caster’s MP Effect fails and has no effect.

Failure: The Caster’s MP Effect succeeds and takes effect. The Check bonus gained from MP spent doesn’t apply to the MP Effect’s Success and Failure Statements.

Tie: The Caster’s MP Effect fails, has no effect, and you each roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.roll-tables.RollTable.HxkcpDT6pEOIfYUk]{Wild Magic Surge Table}. The effect from the table lasts for 1 Round.

Resources Lost: Whatever the result, each creature still spends all AP, MP, or other resources as normal.

Example: A Caster makes an MP based Spell Attack and a Challenger attempts to stop it. The Caster’s Spell Attack cost 1 AP and 2 MP. The Challenger must spend 1 AP and at least 1 MP to attempt to stop the Spell Attack. The Caster makes an Attack Check (since its a Spell Attack) and the Challenger makes a Spell Check. The Caster gets a +4 bonus to their Attack Check (2 MP spent) and the Challenger gets a +2 bonus to their Spell Check (1 MP spent). Should the Caster win the Contest, their MP Effect takes effect but they don’t include the +4 bonus from MP spent to determine if they score a Heavy Hit or higher.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to throw an object or a creature you have Grappled.

Object

The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table. For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically.

Throw at a Target: When you throw an object at a target it’s considered a Ranged Martial Attack with an Improvised Weapon against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The target takes 1 damage of a type determined by the GM.

  • Miss: The thrown object continues traveling up to your maximum throwing distance.

DC Tip: When you throw Weapons (including an Improvised Weapon) that lack the Toss or Thrown Properties as part of an Attack, you make the Attack with DisADV.

Creature

Make an Athletics Check against the target’s Might Save.

  • Check Success: The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table (ending the Grapple).

For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically upward.

Throw at a Target: When you throw the creature at a target, you compare your Athletics Check against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The thrown creature and the target share the Collision damage (each take half).

  • Miss: The thrown creature continues traveling to your maximum throwing distance.

Throwing Table

Size

Spaces Thrown

2+ Sizes smaller

2 x Might

1 Size smaller

Might

Same Size

1/2 Might

Larger

Impossible

Example: A Medium sized creature with a Might of 0 (minimum Might of 1) could throw a Tiny object up to 2 Spaces, a Small object 1 Space, and Medium object 1 Space (rounded up from 0.5). Conversely, if that creature had a Might of 6, they could throw a Tiny object 12 Spaces, a Small object 6 Spaces, and a Medium object 3 Spaces.

System Info: In the DC20 system, throwing is not a basic action and has its own section in the rules. For automation purposes, I added it to the actions.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to Hide from 1 or more creatures that can’t see you (Unseen).

Make a Stealth Check against the opposing creature’(s) Passive Awareness.

Success: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat (making you Unseen and Unheard by them).

You remain Hidden until you make a noise louder than a whisper, make an Attack, cast a Spell with a Verbal Component, or a creature takes the Search Action and successfully locates you.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

system.description: You can spend 1 or more AP to cast a Spell that you know. If the Spell has a Mana Point requirement, you must spend that much MP to cast the Spell.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to make an Attack Check Contested by the target's Athletics, Acrobatics, or Trickery Check (target’s choice).

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

A creature with 4 AP can take the Move Action to move 1 Space, take the Attack Action, move 1 Space again (2 total Spaces), take the Attack Action again, and then move 2 Spaces again (4 total Spaces).

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to push a creature within 1 Space of you. Make an Athletics Check contested by the target’s Martial Check.

Success: You push the creature 1 Space away from you or to its left or right.

Success (each 5): Push up to 1 additional Space.

Knock Prone: After the result, you can choose to reduce the total distance the target is pushed by 1 Space to knock them Prone instead.

DC Tip: If you shove a creature into a target, follow the rules for hitting the target in the Collision section.

You can spend 1 AP to grant another creature a d8 Help Die that lasts until the start of your next turn. Upon granting the Help Die, you must declare which creature you’re Helping and the type of Check you’ll be aiding them with while meeting the following conditions:

  • Attack: You declare 1 target for the Attack. You must be within 1 Space of the Attacker or the target of the Attack. While the Help Die lasts, it can be added to an Attack made against the target.

  • Skill or Trade Check: You declare a type of Skill or Trade Check. You describe how you’re Helping them and must do so with a Skill or Trade that you have at least 1 Mastery Level in. You can use the same Skill or Trade or a different one.

The Help Die can only be used to aid the type of Check declared and disappears once it is used.

Multiple Help Penalty: Once you take the Help Action, each time you take the Help Action again before the end of your turn, your Help Die decays by 1 step, to a minimum of a d4 (d8 | d6 | d4). These Help Dice only decay when using the Help Action. Help Die granted by other sources (such as the Sword Maneuver) decay independently of any Help Dice grant through the Help Action.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to recall or discern some information about a creature that you can see or hear. Make a DC 10 Knowledge Check.

Success: You learn a piece of lore about the creature.

Success (each 5): You learn 1 creature statistic (PD, AD, Attacks, Abilities, Resistances, Vulnerabilities, Immunities, etc.).

If you move at least 2 Spaces in a straight line, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Tackle a creature that’s your size or smaller.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the target's Martial Check.

Success: You Grapple the target and you both move 1 Space in the same direction you were moving then immediately fall Prone.

Success (each 5): +1 Space moved.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to move through a Space occupied by a hostile creature that’s within 1 size of you.

Make a contested Martial Check against the target.

Success: You can move through the creature’s Space as if it were Difficult Terrain (Slowed while moving through the area).

Success (5): You can move through the creature's space without it counting as Difficult Terrain.

You must spend 1 AP at the start of each of your turns to Sustain an effect until the start of your next turn.

You can't Sustain an effect while Dazed. When you become Dazed, any effect you’re Sustaining ends. You can choose to end the effect at anytime for free.

Outside of Combat: You can only Sustain 1 effect at a time outside of Combat. If you attempt to Sustain a second effect while already Sustaining a first, the first effect immediately ends.

Melee Martial Attack vs PD

1 Slashing

Ranged Martial Attack vs PD

15/45 Spaces

1 Piercing

You can spend 1 AP to perform 1 of the following object interactions:

  • Drink a Potion or administer a Potion to another creature.

  • Attempt to lock or unlock a Lock.

  • Make a Trickery Check to activate or disable a trap or other mechanism.

  • Transfer an item to or from another creature (only 1 of the two creatures spends 1 AP).

You can spend 1 AP to make a Trickery Check Contested by the target’s Insight Check.

Success: The next Attack against the target before the start of your next turn has ADV and deals +1 damage.

effects.name: Feint
effects.description: The next Attack against you has ADV and deals +1 damage.

system.description:
Area Martial Attack vs AD

10 Spaces, 3 Space Sphere

1 Piercing

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to taunt a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Influence Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Taunted by you for 1 Round.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to intimidate a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Intimidation Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Intimidated by you until the end of your next turn.

When you perform a Jump, you can spend 1 AP to increase your Jump Distance for the Jump.

Make a DC 10 Acrobatics Check.

  • Failure: Your Jump Distance increases by 1.

  • Success: Your Jump Distance increases by 2.

  • Success (each 5): +1 Jump Distance.

DC Tip: Remember, your Long Jump Distance equals your Jump Distance in Spaces, and your High Jump distance equals your Jump Distance in feet.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to locate 1 or more Hidden creatures and concealed objects within your Line of Sight.

  • Hidden Creatures: You attempt to locate any Hidden creatures in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the Stealth Check of any Hidden creatures.

    Success: You know the location of any Hidden creature whose Stealth Check you beat until the end of your turn. Knowing a creature’s location does not grant you the ability to see it.

  • Hidden Objects: You attempt to locate any Hidden objects in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the DC to discover any concealed objects (such as traps, secret doors, or hidden items).

    Success: You discover the location of any Hidden object whose discovery DC you beat.

Without obstruction, a PC’s line of sight could reach the horizon. You might ask your PC to make their Awareness Check with DisADV to detect a creature that’s far away, such as beyond 10 Spaces.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to beguile a Beast that can see or hear you. Make an Animal Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The animal is Taunted by you for 1 minute (Repeated Save) or until you target it with a harmful Attack, Spell, or other effect.

Success (5): It’s also Impaired.

Success (10): It’s also Charmed.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to hide an object on yourself or in nearby foliage, debris, or decor to render it Hidden.

Make a contested Trickery Check against the Passive Awareness of creatures that can see you.

Success: The object is Hidden from any creature whose Passive Awareness you beat.

You can spend 1 AP to touch a creature and tend to its wounds.

Make a DC 10 Medicine Check.

Success: You stop its Bleeding or Stabilize it (your choice).

Success (each 5): The creature gains +1 Temp HP.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to discern the course of actions a creature might take on its next turn.

Make an Insight Check contested by the target’s Trickery or Influence Check (its choice).

Success: You learn the target’s emotional state and whether it plans to make an Attack, cast a Spell, or flee combat during its next turn.

Success (5): You know who the creature is likely to target with a harmful ability.

Success (10): You know which ability the creature plans to use.

If the target is a PC, its player tells you what the creature plans to do. If the target is an NPC, the GM tells you what the creature plans to do.

Lore: Nightmare, a Fiendborn Wizard, is the crowd control of The Shadows, a rather disreputable adventuring group. Even his bedfellows are a bit creeped out by him.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

effects.description: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat.

system.description: You can spend 1 or more AP to cast a Spell that you know. If the Spell has a Mana Point requirement, you must spend that much MP to cast the Spell.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

You have an arcane focus that grants you ADV on Spell Attacks. You lose this bonus if you’re Disarmed.

System Tip: Just disable this feature if you are disarmed.

effects.name: Arcane Focus
effects.description: You have an arcane focus that grants you ADV on Spell Attacks. You lose this bonus if you’re Disarmed.

system.description: For the purpose of the Spell Duels, your MP Effects have a +2 bonus to their Check.

Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to make an Attack Check Contested by the target's Athletics, Acrobatics, or Trickery Check (target’s choice).

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

system.description:
When a creature uses a MP effect, you can attempt to Spell Duel it with a +2 bonus to your Check.

During a Spell Duel, the creature casting the Spell is referred to as the Caster and the opposing creature is referred to as the Challenger.

If multiple creatures attempt to initiate a Spell Duel, one of them must be chosen as the Challenger, while the others assist them through Combo Casting. Additional creatures can also choose to assist the Caster when a Spell Duel is initiated (see Combo Casting for more information).

Trigger: You see an MP Effect being cast, provided the Caster or a target is within 5 Spaces of you.

Reaction: You can spend an amount of AP and 1 or more MP (up to your Mana Spend Limit) to attempt to stop the Caster’s MP Effect. The amount of AP spent is equal to the base AP cost of the MP Effect you are trying to stop.

Contest

Make a Spell Check contested by the Spell Check or Spell Attack that the Caster makes to cast their MP Effect.

If the Caster’s MP Effect doesn’t require a Check, they have to make a Spell Check as a Reaction as part of the Spell Duel.

For the purposes of the Spell Duel, the Caster and Challenger each gain a bonus to their Check equal to twice the MP spent they spent respectively.

Beta Note: Since enemies don’t have MP costs for their MP Effects you can assume that their MP Effects cost an amount of MP equal to half their Level (rounded up).

Outcomes

Success: The Caster’s MP Effect fails and has no effect.

Failure: The Caster’s MP Effect succeeds and takes effect. The Check bonus gained from MP spent doesn’t apply to the MP Effect’s Success and Failure Statements.

Tie: The Caster’s MP Effect fails, has no effect, and you each roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.roll-tables.RollTable.HxkcpDT6pEOIfYUk]{Wild Magic Surge Table}. The effect from the table lasts for 1 Round.

Resources Lost: Whatever the result, each creature still spends all AP, MP, or other resources as normal.

Example: A Caster makes an MP based Spell Attack and a Challenger attempts to stop it. The Caster’s Spell Attack cost 1 AP and 2 MP. The Challenger must spend 1 AP and at least 1 MP to attempt to stop the Spell Attack. The Caster makes an Attack Check (since its a Spell Attack) and the Challenger makes a Spell Check. The Caster gets a +4 bonus to their Attack Check (2 MP spent) and the Challenger gets a +2 bonus to their Spell Check (1 MP spent). Should the Caster win the Contest, their MP Effect takes effect but they don’t include the +4 bonus from MP spent to determine if they score a Heavy Hit or higher.
